_metadata.description: Qianfan (Thousand Sails / G60 Starlink) constellation orbital parameters compiled from Wikipedia and SpaceNews.
_metadata.chinese_name: 千帆星座 (Qiānfān xīngzuò, 'Thousand Sails Constellation')
notes: Phase 1: 1,296 satellites; 648 to be deployed by end of 2025 for regional coverage.
First launch: 6 August 2024 (18 satellites, polar orbit).
Operates in Ku, Q, and V bands.
Detailed per-shell orbital parameters (altitude, inclination breakdown) not fully disclosed publicly.
All launches so far have been to polar orbits.
Wikipedia launch data does not specify altitude or inclination for individual Qianfan launches.
Estimated operational altitude ~1,200 km based on ITU filings (comparable to OneWeb and Guowang higher shell).

shells.notes: All launches to date have been to polar orbits. Altitude estimated ~1,200 km based on ITU filings. Exact inclination not specified in Wikipedia data. Launched primarily using Long March 6A (18 sats) and Long March 8 (18 sats).

future_shells.description: The full 15,000-satellite constellation will likely include multiple altitude shells and inclinations, but detailed shell breakdown is not publicly available.

shells.notes: Altitude estimated; may differ from actual operational altitude.
